title: marsileaceae
intro: The Marsileaceae rank is a family of plants, specifically a group of aquatic or semi-aquatic flowering plants that are commonly known as water clovers or four-leaf clovers. Members of this family are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide, and are characterized by their unique, four-lobed leaves and submerged rhizomes. The Marsileaceae family is relatively small, consisting of around 70 species across six genera, and is often found growing in slow-moving bodies of water, such as rivers, lakes, and ponds. These plants are often valued for their ability to thrive in a variety of aquatic environments and are sometimes used in aquatic landscaping.
